description: This recipe is one of our favorite fancy breakfasts. It has many ingredients, but it’s not so difficult and long to cook. Dutch baby together with [Café de olla](/recipes/cafe-de-olla/) and our [Good vibes playlist](https://music.apple.com/de/playlist/good-vibes-only/pl.u-2aoqXKzfG66mb65?l=en-GB) is the recipe for a perfect slow Sunday.
source: This recipe is based on the “Double apple Dutch baby” recipe from “Half baked harvest every day” by Tieghan Gerard

steps.text: In a medium bowl, whisk together all dry ingredients: <Ingredient name="flour" modifier="wholewheat" />, <Ingredient name="all-purpose flour"  />, <Ingredient name="cinnamon" modifier="ground" />, <Ingredient name="ground cardamom"  />, and <Ingredient name="salt"  />.

In a mixer bowl, beat together <Ingredient name="egg"  />, <Ingredient name="milk"  />, <Ingredient name="maple syrup"  />, <Ingredient name="vanilla extract"  />, on high speed with a whisk attachment for about 1 minute.
Add dry ingredients to the mixer bowl, and mix util all ingredients are combined.
Quarter the apple, remove the core, and thinly slice.

Melt <Ingredient name="butter"  /> in a medium-size cast iron skillet over medium heat. Add the apple, and cook occasionally stirring, until the butter starts to brown and apple starts to caramelize, for about 15 minutes.
Transfer the apples to a plate, but leave the butter in the skillet.
Pour the batter to the hot skillet, arrange half of the apples on top of the batter, stash the rest.
Put the skillet into the oven, and bake uncovered for about 15 minutes, until the Dutch baby is puffed up and browned on top. (Do not open the oven — it may deflate the Dutch baby.)
Serve topped with the remaining apples, a drizzle of maple syrup, and fresh berries.

title: Dutch baby
